content.problemStatement:
Chef loves graphs. To express his love, he defines a function f(G,x,y)f(G, x, y)f(G,x,y) for an undirected connected graph GGG with weighted edges and two of its vertices xxx, yyy. f(G,x,y)f(G, x, y)f(G,x,y) is defined as the minimum of weights of all paths in GGG between the vertices xxx and yyy, where the weight of a path is the maximum of weights of edges in this path.

Chef has two connected graphs G1G_1G1​ and G2G_2G2​. Each of these graphs has NNN vertices (labelled 111 through NNN) and MMM edges. Calculate S=∑i=1N−1∑j=i+1Nf(G1,i,j)⋅f(G2,i,j)S = \sum_{i=1}^{N-1} \sum_{j=i+1}^N f(G_1, i, j) \cdot f(G_2, i, j)S=∑i=1N−1​∑j=i+1N​f(G1​,i,j)⋅f(G2​,i,j) modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: G1G_1G1​ and G2G_2G2​ are connected graphs
G1G_1G1​ and G2G_2G2​ are identical graphs
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. Each of these lines contains three space-separated integers uuu, vvv and www denoting that vertices uuu and vvv in G1G_1G1​ are connected by an edge with weight www.
MMM more lines follow. Each of these lines contains three space-separated integers uuu, vvv and www denoting that vertices uuu and vvv in G2G_2G2​ are connected by an edge with weight www.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the sum SSS modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
